questions.question: What is the 'Golden Rule of Forms' mentioned in the lesson?

options.text: More fields provide better quality leads and should always be used.
Fewer fields result in higher conversion rates.
Every form must ask for a phone number.
Forms should only be placed on the 'Contact Us' page.

questions.explanation: Every field added to a form acts as friction, reducing the likelihood of completion. Starting with fewer fields (like just email) maximizes conversion.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key design element that distinguishes a high-converting landing page from a standard homepage?

options.text: A detailed navigation menu with links to 'About', 'Blog', and 'Services'.
A 'one-way street' approach with zero navigation and a single CTA.
As much text as possible to explain every feature of the business.
Multiple competing offers to give the visitor more choices.

questions.explanation: Landing pages succeed by removing distractions and focusing the user on one specific action.

questions.question: Why is '7 Cold Email Subject Lines that Got a 40% Reply Rate' a better lead magnet than 'The Ultimate Guide to Marketing'?

options.text: It is longer and more impressive.
It focuses on specificity and immediate value rather than broad, overwhelming education.
It costs more to produce.
It doesn't require an email address to download.

questions.explanation: Specificity and immediate value (the 'Aha!' moment) are the keys to successful lead magnets that build trust quickly.

questions.question: When is an 'Exit-Intent' popup triggered?

options.text: Exactly 30 seconds after the page loads.
When the user scrolls to the bottom of the page.
When the user moves their cursor toward the browser's close button or address bar.
Only when a user clicks 'Unsubscribe'.

questions.explanation: Exit-intent technology captures users who are about to leave your site by offering them one last value proposition.

questions.question: Which capture mechanism is describe as 'the respectful ask' and is usually embedded within content?

options.text: Sticky Bar
Inline Form
Full-screen Takeover
Timed Popup

questions.explanation: Inline forms are non-intrusive and appear naturally within the flow of reading content, often yielding high-quality leads.

questions.question: Think about your current lead magnet or the one you plan to create. Evaluate it against the three principles: Specificity, Immediate Value, and Strategic Alignment. How could you refine the title and scope of your lead magnet to better convert visitors? Describe the 'Aha!' moment it provides.
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the student's lead magnet concept. Check if it's too broad or misaligned with their core offer. Provide a 'Specific + Result' headline suggestion based on their input.
